target_game.series_state_entering_game: Cleveland leads 3-2 after Game 5 road win

source_files_compounded: Conversation-supplied Game 5 final/postmortem inputs: CLE 117, DET 113; CLE TT over 105.5 hit; CLE +4.5 position won; Tobias Harris 8+ rebounds lost

full_game.cle_moneyline_range: -159 to -177, with many books around -168 to -175
full_game.spread_market: CLE -3.5 to -4.5, with Kalshi ladder showing CLE win by over 3.5 at -121
full_game.total_market: Mostly 209.5 to 210.5, down from prior Game 5 range near 211.5 to 212.5

team_totals.cle_team_total: 106.5 to 107.5; Kalshi shows CLE over 107.5 at -108 and lower ladder 104.5 too expensive around -161
team_totals.det_team_total: 102.5 to 103.5 at books; Kalshi shows DET over 105.5 at +113

early_locked_position.model_alignment: Supported as the primary Game 6 compound-position from Game 5 correction and Cleveland home response.

duncan_robinson.status: Questionable for Game 6 after missing Game 5 with lower-back soreness
model_effect_if_out: More creation burden shifts to Cade Cunningham
Daniss Jenkins, Caris LeVert, Kevin Huerter, and Ausar Thompson minute/usage paths become more sensitive to availability
Tobias Harris may gain floor time stability but not automatically rebound conversion because Cleveland frontcourt controlled Game 5 glass

detroit.kevin_huerter: Questionable/rotation-sensitive; if limited, Detroit perimeter scoring depth remains fragile.
detroit.caris_levert: Questionable/rotation-sensitive; if active, adds bench creation but also redistributes rebound and assist opportunities away from Harris/Jenkins.

cleveland.health_context: No Cleveland inactive concern was flagged in the provided lineup screen.

game5_edge_results.market: No - Detroit wins by over 4.5 points / Cleveland +4.5 equivalent

game5_final_anchor.model_lesson: Cleveland spread/team-total reads were validated. Tobias Harris rebound ladder had supportive minutes/recent chance logic but failed due to Cleveland frontcourt control and Detroit rebound distribution instability.

offensive_profile: Game 5 showed Cleveland can clear team total even after Detroit first-half pressure if creation quality stabilizes.
Harden plus Mobley assist hub structure generated multiple assisted paint and perimeter scoring paths.
Max Strus and Sam Merrill spacing tails matter more than pure starter scoring projections.
Mitchell remains the scoring gravity/takeover tail, but not the only path to over outcomes.
rebounding_profile: Cleveland frontcourt rebound pressure strengthened across Game 5.
Dean Wade defensive rebounding spike is a small but relevant long-board stabilizer.
Allen/Mobley combination remains the strongest anti-Detroit second-chance defense and best prop derivative angle.
turnover_profile: Cleveland still had live-ball turnover risk versus Detroit pressure, but Game 5 showed enough second-half correction to overcome it.
If Cleveland first-half turnovers are under control, Game 6 CLE spread and team-total edges strengthen materially.

offensive_profile: Cade Cunningham is the primary pressure valve and late-clock offense source.
Detroit becomes more Cade-dependent when Duncan Robinson is out or limited.
Detroit team-total overs require either Cade scoring burst, fast-break pressure, or unsustainably high early shooting.
Detroit half-court spacing is less stable when Robinson/Huerter/LeVert statuses are uncertain.
rebounding_profile: Duren remains high-ceiling but volatile versus Cleveland size.
Tobias Harris rebounding cannot be treated as a clean ladder despite strong prior tracking because Cleveland frontcourt suppression was validated in Game 5.
Ausar Thompson can collect event rebounds, but his offensive role volatility makes rebound ladders thin unless price is favorable.
turnover_profile: Detroit can create pressure but also loses offensive stability when Cade carries too much initiation burden.
If Detroit turnovers stay low, their spread/live moneyline stays dangerous; if turnovers rise, Cleveland margin expansion becomes live.

evan_mobley_9_plus.action: Playable early if still +110 or better

jarrett_allen_8_plus.action: Playable, below Mobley due to Duren/Allen minute and foul sensitivity

jalen_duren_10_plus.action: Live/ghost unless price improves or Cleveland missed-shot volume rises

tobias_harris_7_plus_or_8_plus.action: Do not chase. Require live rebound-chance validation before re-entry.

james_harden_7_plus.action: Watchlist or small secondary if correlation exposure is acceptable

cade_cunningham_9_plus.action: Ghost/live only; Detroit must shoot efficiently off Cade creation

cade_cunningham_30_plus.action: Better live if Detroit trails and Cade usage spikes

tobias_harris_20_plus.action: Do not chase Game 5 scoring burst

james_harden_20_plus.action: Playable only if avoiding Harden assists; do not stack both heavily

workflow_directives_game6: Use Game 5 Cleveland correction and frontcourt control as the highest-weight recent form input.
Do not overweight Detroit season record or home Game 5 favorite status now that venue flips to Cleveland.
CLE -3.5 is the primary position; avoid over-clustering into CLE team total unless line falls to 106.5 or price improves.
Prioritize Cleveland big rebound props over Detroit wing rebound ladders.
For Tobias Harris, require live validation: 4+ rebound chances by early 2Q or 6+ by halftime before considering re-entry.
Treat Duncan Robinson status as a spacing toggle: OUT/limited supports CLE spread and DET under; active and shooting well increases DET team-total volatility.

edge_ranking_game6_early.market: CLE -3.5 equivalent / Cleveland wins by over 3.5

edge_ranking_game6_early.price_seen: No direct under price from Kalshi screen; DET over 105.5 +113 implies under expensive
